name: add-discord
tagline: Copies a Discord channel adapter from a branch, wires bot credentials via Discord's own API, and restarts the service to enable a Discord.
verdict: Solid, idempotent, well-troubleshot integration flow with sane credential handling (secret prompt, .env, no echoing). Weak spots: description doesn't state a trigger phrase, no negative-trigger scope callout among sibling add-* skills, jq/curl used with no declared prerequisite, and no version/changelog signal.

highlights.summary: description 'Add Discord bot channel integration via Chat SDK.' says what it does but names no triggering phrase or situation.
No 'NOT for'/out-of-scope pointer anywhere, despite implied sibling channel skills (Slack, etc.) that could be confused with this one.
jq/curl invoked directly ('| jq -er ...' resolving the DM channel) with no requirements section or availability check anywhere in the skill.
